div.head: [Telegram from Mr. Pruyn to Mr. Seward.]

dateline.placeName: Legation of the United States in Japan, Kanagawa,

div.p: Sir: Two British officers, in citizen’s dress, Major Baldwin and Lieutena nt Bird, of the 20th regiment, have been murdered by Japanese at Kamakma, within treaty limits. Government declares itself anxious for their arrest, claiming it an act of hostility to itself.
I have the honor to be your most obedient servant,

closer.signed: ROBT. H. PRUYN, Minister Resident of the United States in Japan.

postscript.p: Hon. William H. Seward. Secretary of State, &c., &c., &c.
